channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: NATO clarifies comments that four missing US soldiers had died during training in Lithuania
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — NATO on Wednesday clarified comments that Secretary-General Mark Rutte made earlier in the day, when he suggested that four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania had died, even though the U.S. Army said their fate was not yet confirmed.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korea is in an uphill battle to contain massive wildfires as the death toll rises to 26
item.description: Multiple wildfires raging across South Korea’s southern regions for days have killed 26 people and destroyed more than 300 structures, as thousands of personnel and dozens of helicopters have mobilized to battle the the county’s worst-ever blazes.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea’s centuries-old Gounsa temple is left in ruins by unprecedented wildfires
item.description: Wildfires in South Korea's southern regions have left 24 people dead and destroyed more than 300 structures, including large parts of an ancient Buddhist temple complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of breaking ceasefire terms protecting energy sites
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine have accused each other of breaking the terms of a tentative U.S-brokered deal to pause strikes on energy infrastructure, underscoring the challenges to negotiating a broader peace in the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel orders more evacuations and Palestinians stage rare anti-Hamas protests
item.description: The Israeli military on Wednesday ordered the evacuation of parts of Gaza City as it steps up its renewed offensive against Hamas after breaking the ceasefire last week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron says a proposed European force for Ukraine could 'respond' if attacked by Russia
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron says that a proposed European armed force for possible deployment in Ukraine in tandem with an eventual peace deal could “respond” to a Russian attack if Moscow launched one.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sudanese army surrounds Khartoum airport in battle for capital, military sources say
item.description: The Sudanese army is encircling Khartoum airport as it battles to oust its rival Rapid Support Forces from its final foothold in Sudan's capital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US soldiers missing in Lithuania: Pentagon
item.description: The Army said Wednesday that four U.S. soldiers are missing in Lithuania. The U.S. Army Europe and Africa said in a press release that the missing soldiers, part of the Army’s 1st Brigade, 3rd Infantry Division, were close to the Lithuanian city of Pabradė within a training area. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Australia focuses foreign aid spending on Pacific region as US slashes aid programs
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia plans to spend a larger share of its foreign aid on its neighbors in Asia and the Pacific islands after the United States announced major cuts to development and humanitarian aid abroad.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea’s truth commission says government responsible for fraud and abuse in foreign adoptions
item.description: South Korea’s truth commission has concluded that the government bears responsibility for facilitating a foreign adoption program rife with fraud and abuse, driven by efforts to reduce welfare costs and enabled by private agencies that often manipulated children’s backgrounds and origins.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Two military jets collide in midair stunt, crash to ground in fiery explosion
item.description: Two Alpha jets collided midair during a training exercise in France before crashing to the ground and exploding in a massive fireball, dramatic video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Hamas protests break out in Gaza Strip over demands to end war
item.description: Palestinians protested in northern Gaza, where they reportedly demanded an end to the war in anti-Hamas protests. The last major protest against the terror group occurred in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says Russia, Ukraine agree to ‘eliminate the use of force’ in Black Sea
item.description: The White House announced Tuesday it came to an agreement with Ukraine and Russia to “eliminate the use of force” in the Black Sea, as well as end strikes on energy facilities, marking one of the more significant steps the Trump administration has seen toward a stop in fighting.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: South Africa president says persecution of whites a 'false narrative' as Musk repeats genocide claim
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South African President Cyril Ramaphosa said Monday the claim that white people are being persecuted in his country is a “completely false narrative.” It was his latest attempt to push back against allegations made by U.S.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: South Korean PM Han Duck-soo reinstated as acting president after impeachment overturned
item.description: South Korea's Constitutional Court overturned acting President Han Duck-soo's impeachment, reinstating the nation's No. 2 official as acting leader on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist arrested, attacked by locals after scaling world-famous sacred Mayan temple, video shows
item.description: Video shows a tourist scaling the steps of Mexico's Temple of Kukulcan as thousands of visitors flocked to the site to witness the spring equinox last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oleg Gordievsky, Britain's most valuable Cold War spy inside the KGB, dies at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Oleg Gordievsky, a Soviet KGB officer who helped change the course of the Cold War by covertly passing secrets to Britain, has died. He was 86. Gordievsky died March 4 in England, where he had lived since defecting in 1985.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 2 convicted in US of Iran-backed plot to kill journalist critical of Tehran
item.description: Prosecutors said Iran’s elite Revolutionary Guard Corps paid Amirov and Omarov $500,000 for the botched hit.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: European countries warn against US travel amid immigration crackdown
item.description: The UK and Germany updated travel advisories after citizens were arrested and detained when attempting to enter the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Strikes in Gaza kill 85 overnight, bringing the total since Israel broke ceasefire to nearly 600
item.description: DEIR-AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Local health officials said Israeli strikes killed at least 85 Palestinians across he Gaza Strip overnight and into Thursday, bringing the total to nearly 600 killed since Israel shattered a truce that had facilitated the release of more than two dozen hostages and b
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New skeletons challenge assumptions about ancient Egypt
item.description: The skeletons found laid to rest alongside nobles has challenged assumptions about Egyptian burial practices.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation
